meaning: n.古代不列颠或威尔士的王侯,首领
examples: Pendragon was the name of several Kings of the Britons.
He was known as Uther Pendragon after being inspired by a dragon-shaped comet.
Fittingly for his formidable reputation, he was also known as Arthur Pendragon.
The Pendragon Legend is published by the Pushkin Press, which has taken upon itself the task of exposing readers to European writers neglected in the Anglophone world.
